guide.exampleCreated: Example note created — find it in the file tree.
guide.title: Feature Guide

ai.setup: Connect a provider (API key or local CLI) in Settings › AI.
ai.what: The master switch for all AI: summarize, compose, and rewrite actions in the editor, plus every AI sub-feature. Nothing runs until you invoke it.
ai.where: Editor › AI menu, and the command palette.

aiMetaSuggestions.what: One-click metadata: suggests tags and properties for the current note; you approve before anything is written.

aiTemplates.what: Your own prompt templates become custom actions in the AI menu.
aiTemplates.where: Editor › AI menu; manage the templates in Settings › AI.

ambientAi.cost: Runs on every pause while you write, so it costs AI tokens continuously.
ambientAi.setup: A separate opt-in: both the AI master switch and the ambient toggle (Settings › AI or Settings › Features) must be on.
ambientAi.what: After an edit settles, suggests links and metadata for the note you're writing — quietly, in the background.
ambientAi.where: Suggestions appear as a dismissible strip in the editor.

backlinks.what: Which notes link here — plus unlinked mentions of this note's title that you can turn into links.

blockRefs.setup: After enabling, accept the reindex offer in Settings › Features so existing ids are found.

syntax.marker: End a line with a space + ^id (letters/digits, 4–32 chars) to give the block an address.
syntax.reference: Embeds the block with that id; typing (( opens suggestions.

blockRefs.what: Give any block a permanent ^id address, then link or embed exactly that block elsewhere.
blockRefs.where: In the editor; the note header has a copy-block-reference button.

calendar.what: Month, week, and day views over your own events; events are stored as Markdown in the vault.

calendarSync.what: Show events from Google or Outlook accounts alongside your local calendar (read-only).
calendarSync.where: Events appear in the Calendar and Today views.

syntax.basic: First line of a blockquote; the type picks the color and icon.
syntax.titled: Optional custom title after the type; unknown types render as note.

callouts.what: Styled callout boxes from plain blockquotes (Obsidian-compatible). Types: note, tip, info, warning, danger, success, question, quote, caution, important, error.
callouts.where: In the editor — the blockquote renders as a colored box.

canvas.what: Freeform spatial boards mixing sticky notes and embedded vault notes. Boards are portable .canvas files.

cheatsheet.title: Shortcut cheatsheet
cheatsheet.what: An overlay listing every keyboard shortcut with its current binding — it updates when you rebind keys.
cheatsheet.where: Open it with its shortcut; rebind everything in Settings › Keybindings.

syntax.fence: Name the language after the opening fence to pick the grammar.

codeHighlight.what: Syntax colors in fenced code blocks. Language grammars load on demand, so unused ones cost nothing.
codeHighlight.where: In the editor, in any fenced code block.

commandPalette.title: Command palette
commandPalette.what: Every command in one fuzzy-searchable list: switch views, create notes from templates, run plugin and AI actions.
commandPalette.where: Opens as an overlay on top of whatever you're doing.

dailyNotes.what: One journal note per day, created the first time you open it.

syntax.slash: The block menu on an empty line: headings, lists, tables, images, and more.
syntax.tag: Tags the note; tags show in the sidebar's tag browser and are searchable.

editorBasics.title: Writing basics
editorBasics.what: Notes are plain Markdown files. Format with the toolbar, the / menu, or Markdown itself; paste or drop images straight into a note.
editorBasics.where: Every note tab; the toolbar sits above the note.

entityGraph.cost: Extraction spends AI tokens whenever notes are (re)indexed — that's why it stays a separate opt-in even when AI is on.
entityGraph.what: Extracts people, places, and topics from your notes into a browsable entity graph.

gitSync.setup: Its switch lives with git's own settings: set author identity and an optional remote in Settings › Sync. The Features row flips the same switch.
gitSync.what: Version the vault with git and sync it to a remote — full history, diffs, and conflict handling.
gitSync.where: Settings › Sync, and the sync status in the sidebar.

graphView.what: An interactive map of the links between your notes. Drag, zoom, and click through to any note.

icsSubscriptions.setup: Add feed URLs under Settings › Calendar.
icsSubscriptions.what: Follow read-only .ics feeds (public holidays, team calendars, private iCal links).
icsSubscriptions.where: Events appear in the Calendar and Today views.

syntax.block: Displayed (block) math on its own line.
syntax.inline: Inline math. No space just inside the $ signs — so $5 and $10 stay plain text.

math.what: Renders TeX math with KaTeX. The library loads only when a note uses it.
math.where: In the editor — rendered in place.

syntax.fence: A code block with the language set to mermaid renders as a diagram.

mermaid.what: Turns mermaid code blocks into rendered diagrams: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, gantt charts, and more.
mermaid.where: In the editor — the diagram renders in place. The library loads only when a note uses it.

outline.what: The note's heading structure as a clickable table of contents.

p2pSync.setup: Pair your devices once in Settings › Sync.
p2pSync.what: End-to-end encrypted sync directly between your devices — no server in between.

pdfAnnotate.what: Read PDFs in the app, highlight passages, and link highlights to the notes that cite them.
pdfAnnotate.where: Command palette › open PDF; annotations live in the viewer's side panel.

plugins.what: Vault-local JavaScript plugins, sandboxed in Web Workers, that add commands to the palette.
plugins.where: Manage them in Settings › Plugins; their commands appear in the command palette.

syntax.frontmatter: Properties live between --- fences at the very top of the note.
syntax.number: A number property — sortable and comparable in queries.
syntax.relation: A relation: points at another note by wikilink; query it with project:[[Launch]].

properties.what: Typed key–value metadata in a note's frontmatter — text, numbers, checkboxes, lists, and relations to other notes — editable in a structured panel.
properties.where: The properties panel at the top of a note; the raw YAML stays editable.

syntax.alias: A note alias contains the text.
syntax.folder: Note is in the folder or any subfolder.
syntax.fulltext: Full-text match on body, title, and tags; quote multiple words as a phrase.
syntax.hasFacet: Note has at least one task; has:link and has:backlink work too.
syntax.hasProp: Note has a frontmatter property with that name.
syntax.propEquals: Frontmatter property equals a value (any property key works).
syntax.relation: Typed relation points at the note with that title.
syntax.sort: Order by title, path, modified, created, or any property key; :asc/:desc picks the direction.
syntax.sortSimilarity: Order by semantic similarity to a phrase (needs the semantic index).
syntax.taskDue: Note has a task due before the date; task.start works too.
syntax.taskPriority: Note has a task with the priority; task.project: and task.epic: work too.
syntax.taskStatus: Note has a task with that status.
syntax.title: Note title contains the text.
syntax.view: Pick the result view: table, kanban, or calendar.

queryEngine.what: Saved, database-style views over notes and tasks. Write filter terms, pick a table, kanban, or calendar view, and save the query for reuse.

readingMode.title: Reading mode
readingMode.what: A distraction-free, read-only rendering of the current note.

relatedNotes.cost: On-device indexing downloads a ~130 MB model once; an API embedding endpoint works instead.
relatedNotes.setup: Needs the semantic index — build it in Settings › AI.
relatedNotes.what: A panel of notes semantically similar to the one you're reading — no links required.

reminders.what: System notifications for due tasks (@remind) and upcoming calendar events.
reminders.where: Delivered by your OS while the app is running.

search.title: Search
search.what: Full-text search across note titles, bodies, and tags, with highlighted snippets. For fuzzy note-jumping, use the command palette.
search.where: Sidebar › search icon, or the shortcut from anywhere.

tagAutocomplete.what: Suggests your existing tags while you type a #, keeping tag spelling consistent.
tagAutocomplete.where: In the editor — a popup under the cursor.

taskExtract.what: Reads the current note (e.g. meeting minutes) and proposes a task list; you review it before the tasks are inserted.
taskExtract.where: Editor › AI menu or command palette › Extract tasks.

tasks.what: All tasks from all notes in one place — a filterable list and a kanban board. Checkboxes in notes keep working even with this view off.

syntax.checkbox: A checkbox line becomes a task; the heading above it is kept as its section.
syntax.epic: Groups the task under an epic.
syntax.nlDates: Task date fields also accept natural phrases like these and resolve them to real dates.
syntax.priority: Sets the priority: urgent, high, medium, or low.
syntax.project: Groups the task under a project.
syntax.remind: Schedules a reminder at that date and time.
syntax.repeat: Repeats the task: daily, weekly, monthly, yearly, or every N days/weeks/months.
syntax.status: Sets the kanban column (lowercase letters, digits, dashes).

taskTokens.title: Tasks in notes
taskTokens.what: Every Markdown checkbox is a task. @tokens on the same line add dates, priority, kanban status, and project/epic grouping; indented checkboxes become subtasks.
taskTokens.where: In any note; extracted tasks appear in the Tasks and Today views.

todayView.what: Your day on one screen: overdue and due tasks plus today's calendar events.

transclusion.what: Show one note (or a section of it) inside another. Embeds render live, up to three levels deep.
transclusion.where: In the editor — the embed renders in place.

vaultChat.cost: On-device indexing downloads a ~130 MB model once; an API embedding endpoint works instead.
vaultChat.setup: Needs the semantic index — build it in Settings › AI.
vaultChat.what: Ask questions across your whole vault; answers stream in and cite the notes they come from.
vaultChat.where: Command palette or activity rail › chat; docks on the right.

versionHistory.title: Version history
versionHistory.what: Automatic snapshots of every note as you edit. Browse old versions and restore any of them.

voice.cost: Downloads a ~142 MB transcription model on first use; delete it any time under Settings › Features › Stored data.
voice.what: Record audio (e.g. meetings) and transcribe it on this device with Whisper. The transcript becomes a note and can feed task extraction.

weeklyReview.what: An AI-written look back over your week's notes and completed tasks. Without it, the plain digest stays available.

syntax.alias: Links to the note but shows your own text.
syntax.heading: Links to one section of the note.
syntax.link: Links to the note with that title.

wikilinks.title: Wikilinks
wikilinks.what: Link notes by title with double brackets; backlinks and the graph pick the connection up automatically.
wikilinks.where: In the editor — type [[ anywhere in a note and pick from the suggestions.
